title: Slow Clients, Resource Limits, Backpressure, Admission, and Cleanup · Go Retrieval
summary: A delayed handoff reconstructs slow clients, resource limits, backpressure, admission, and cleanup from bounded byte and state evidence, challenges one misconception, and revises the connection resource budget and overload policy. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for connection resource budget and overload policy after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Bound start-line, header, content, trailer, idle, and total-phase time using both progress and absolute budgets.
Bound per-connection buffers, retained slices, field maps, content queues, trailer data, and pooled-object capacity before allocation.
Propagate slow peer writes through bounded queues, cancellation, deadlines, partial output evidence, and producer shutdown.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Separate evidence from confidence · Review Resource Cleanup Proof
steps.instruction:
review case — A review protocol team handles case 1054 in a multi-tenant intake service near its connection and memory ceilings. Build deterministic Go evidence to combine absolute phase time, memory, and output-queue budgets for slow-peer defense; preserve this operating constraint: the maintained Go protocol stack remains the production transfer target; then minimize a fuzz failure and add it to the deterministic regression corpus. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Prove connection, goroutine, timer, buffer, queue, tunnel, temporary data, and metric-cardinality cleanup for every terminal state.

Inspect the Resource Cleanup Proof trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Separate evidence from confidence · Review Resource Cleanup Proof.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for prove connection, goroutine, timer, buffer, queue, tunnel, temporary data, and metric-cardinality cleanup for every terminal state. For Separate evidence from confidence · Review Resource Cleanup Proof, keep Resource Cleanup Proof connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

content.text: For Resource Cleanup Proof, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
